core_mechanism: self-consistent mean-field (self-consistent-field) closure for two mutually interacting populations sharing a common medium, in which each population's local density sources a shared mediating field that sets a density-dependent effective interaction, solved to a fixed point that yields the system's equation of state
brief_reason: The abstract builds a single-fluid relativistic mean-field model where a massive vector mediator produces a density-dependent repulsive coupling between two sectors and the local density of one is fixed self-consistently from the other, generating the equation of state at the mean-field fixed point.
